title: actinocephalus armeria
intro: Actinocephalus armeria, also known as the "São Francisco's Herb", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, specifically in Brazil, and is part of the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Actinocephalus armeria can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
